run_mode: MODE 1 (6 critical/major open; new majors added this run)

patches.find: A local sun at fixed height H ≈ 8,537 km, traveling in a circular path at latitude φ.
patches.replace: A local sun at fixed height H ≈ 5,733 km (the dome's own stated sun altitude, derived from crepuscular ray triangulation), traveling in a circular path at latitude φ.
patches.rationale: MAJOR FACTUAL ERROR: 8,537 km is the firmament apex height H(r=0), not the sun altitude. The dome explicitly places the sun at 5,733 km (inject_ai_layer.py: sun_altitude=5,733 km). The DOCX (build-doc-v4.js) already correctly uses 5,733 km. This is an embarrassing error that any dome defender checking the model parameters would exploit.

patches.find: and instead using the globe formula g = GM / r².
patches.replace: and instead using the globe's empirically measured WGS84 surface gravity values (g ≈ 9.78–9.83 m/s², varying with latitude) — substituting globe data for his own model's prediction rather than deriving gravity from his aetheric circulation equations.
patches.rationale: MAJOR FACTUAL ERROR: The dome does not use g = GM / r² (a point-mass free-fall formula). Section 4.5.8 of this same document correctly identifies the dome using the WGS84 latitude-dependent formula g = 9.7803 − 0.0325·cos(2φ). Citing the wrong formula allows a defender to claim our characterization is a straw man. The corrected text accurately describes what the dome does (adopts globe data) without misidentifying which globe formula.

patches.find: At least six WINs rely on aetheric refraction to avoid falsification: WIN-016 (annual aberration), WIN-017 (stellar parallax), WIN-026 (crepuscular rays), WIN-033 (Sigma Octantis dimness), WIN-056 (solar elevation), WIN-065 (Polaris excess).
patches.replace: At least five WINs rely on aetheric refraction to avoid falsification: WIN-016 (annual aberration), WIN-017 (stellar parallax), WIN-026 (crepuscular rays), WIN-033 (Sigma Octantis dimness), WIN-065 (Polaris excess). WIN-056 uses a different evasion — the author substitutes the globe's 23.45° axial tilt declination formula rather than deriving solar elevation from dome geometry — and is catalogued in Section 4.5 (Self-Contradictions) rather than here.
patches.rationale: WIN-056's verdict is Self-Contradicted (uses globe's declination formula, not dome geometry). Its resolution does not involve aetheric refraction. Including it in the refraction-dependent WIN list mischaracterizes the error type and conflates two distinct evasion strategies. A dome defender could argue 'WIN-056 is in the wrong list' and use that to undermine Section 4.9's analysis.

patches.find: The "P-wave shadow zone" (140–103° from epicenter) is caused by refraction at the liquid core boundary
patches.replace: The P-wave shadow zone (104°–140° from epicenter) is caused by refraction at the liquid core boundary
patches.rationale: Angular range written in reverse order: 140–103° should be 104°–140°. WIN-064's detail_claim states '104°-140°' correctly, and the kill-shot analysis section (line 1092) also uses 104°–140°. The inverted range in the tomography kill-shot section is an internal inconsistency that undermines credibility.

patches.find: Duplicate of WIN-037 with identical threshold and data
patches.replace: Mathematically equivalent to WIN-037 (≥28 nT cumulative per 12 months = ≥28 nT/yr rate); both WINs test SAA-core stations only, not the global dipole — see WIN-037 for full analysis
patches.rationale: ISS-333: Calling WIN-042 a simple 'duplicate' invites a dome defender to argue that a cumulative threshold and a rate threshold are conceptually distinct predictions testing different time behaviors. The revised finding preempts this by explaining the mathematical equivalence explicitly, while noting the shared fundamental flaw (regional not global) and cross-referencing WIN-037.

patches.find: It was part of a batch of five predictions (WIN-035 through WIN-039) all registered on the same date, suggesting systematic data mining from already-published sources rather than prospective prediction.
patches.replace: It was part of a batch of five predictions (WIN-035 through WIN-039) all registered on the same date, suggesting systematic data mining from already-published sources rather than prospective prediction. WIN-042 makes the mathematically equivalent claim framed as an annualized rate (≥28 nT/yr) rather than a 12-month cumulative threshold — the same measurement expressed twice does not constitute two independent confirmations.
patches.rationale: ISS-325: WIN-037's evidence never explicitly cross-references WIN-042's mathematical equivalence. Adding this sentence closes the double-counting argument and prevents a reader from treating WIN-037 and WIN-042 as independent evidence.

patches.find: However, no published formula connects this flow topology to a specific hemispheric decay asymmetry ratio.
patches.replace: However, no published formula connects this flow topology to a specific hemispheric decay asymmetry ratio — meaning a dome defender who argues 'our toroidal aetheric vortex naturally produces hemispheric asymmetry' has not addressed the core objection: without a predicted numerical value derived before measurement, any observed asymmetry ratio will retrospectively 'match' an unspecified topology. The dome needs to commit to a predicted ratio (e.g., NH decays 1.4× faster than SH over the next 10 years) before observation, not after.
patches.rationale: ISS-391: The current text only says 'no formula connects topology to ratio,' which allows a dome defender to say 'we don't need a formula — the asymmetry is qualitatively expected from our model.' The revised text explicitly addresses this counter-argument by requiring a pre-committed quantitative prediction, not just qualitative expectation.

patches.find: The dome has no ontology for extragalactic objects — without one, claims about their redshift behavior are physically meaningless.
patches.replace: The dome has no ontology for extragalactic objects — without one, claims about their redshift behavior are physically meaningless. A further problem: if aetheric propagation causes redshift, then the dome's model must predict a DIFFERENT Hubble constant than standard cosmology — the value of H₀ would encode the aetheric attenuation rate per unit distance, not the cosmic expansion rate. The dome makes no such prediction, instead adopting the same H₀ range (67–74 km/s/Mpc) used by standard cosmology. If the mechanism differs but the prediction is identical, the observation confirms neither mechanism.
patches.rationale: ISS-304: Our current defense only says 'no mechanism.' The stronger argument is that a genuinely different redshift mechanism would require a genuinely different H₀ prediction. By adopting the cosmological H₀ without derivation, the dome's 'aetheric redshift' is indistinguishable from standard expansion — making it non-predictive, not just mechanistically vague.

DEFERRED — ACTION REQUIRED: Run 'node build-scripts/sync-code-analysis.js --apply --workspace' from the clean clone to sync the 8 remaining curmudgeon-reviewed code_analysis tags from review files to wins.json. All 67 curmudgeon reviews are complete; the digest shows 67/67 processed but the integrity report shows only 59/67 synced in wins.json. This is a one-line command requiring no judgment — only execution.

DEFERRED — MANUAL ACTION: SEC-3.1 references the dome's 'Live Power' analysis page classifying 20 domains. This page should be archived to the Wayback Machine and/or saved as a raw-text file in raw-text/ to make our 20-domain classification verifiable. This requires a human to: (1) navigate to the dome's analysis page, (2) submit to archive.org, (3) add the archive URL as a citation in Section 3.1. Cannot be done automatically.

already_resolved_notes.note: WIN-037 detail_evidence already contains: 'PROS-007 was registered 6 March 2026 and confirmed by 12 March 2026 — a 6-day turnaround using WMM2025 data publicly available since December 2024.' The 6-day gap is explicitly documented. Recommend human verify and close.

WIN-042 detail_evidence already contains: 'approximately 15 nT/yr in globally averaged surface intensity (Thébault et al., 2015, IGRF-12, doi:10.1186/s40623-015-0228-9).' The ~15 nT/yr figure is properly cited. Recommend human verify and close.

WIN-055 detail_evidence already contains: 'See also WIN-047, which claims the same distance-redshift relationship using different standard candles — a salami-slicing duplication (Section 6.1, Pattern #4).' ISS-362's requested cross-reference is already present. Recommend human verify and close.

WIN-059 finding and detail_evidence do not call it a 'duplicate of WIN-007.' The finding now correctly says 'Dome's own Kill-Shot Test 6 failing at 39.9% error; axially symmetric geometry cannot predict sector-specific behavior.' Previous patch was applied. Recommend human verify and close.

WIN-059 detail_evidence is now substantive: covers Kill-Shot Test 6 failure (39.9% error), Livermore 2020 flux lobe explanation, axial symmetry argument, and group inflation. ISS-387's requested content is all present. Recommend human verify and close.

WIN-059 detail_evidence already notes: 'The dome's field equation H(r)=8,537·exp(−r/8,619) is axially symmetric — it predicts the same field at all azimuths for a given radius. It has no mechanism to distinguish Siberian sector from any other azimuthal direction.' The vagueness of the dome's claim is documented. Recommend human verify and close.

WIN-063 detail_evidence already includes: '(Note: these simulations are data-constrained numerical models, not purely first-principles derivations — but they are derived from Maxwell's equations and Navier-Stokes, with boundary conditions from seismology.)' CHAOS-7 overstatement has a caveat. Recommend human verify and close.

WIN-063 detail_evidence already states: 'The dome model states the asymmetry ratio "matches" without specifying: (a) what numerical ratio is claimed, (b) over what time period, (c) using what definition of "hemispheric" on a flat disc... or (d) what physical mechanism produces a specific ratio.' All four unspecified items are listed. Recommend human verify and close.

WIN-064 current detail_evidence does not cite a DOI for Gutenberg (1913). It cites only Oldham (1906) with doi:10.1144/GSL.JGS.1906.062.01-04.21. No problematic Gutenberg DOI exists in the current text. ISS-394 appears to reference an older version. Recommend human verify and close.
